I drift through the ventilation ducts of the High Spire, waiting for a royal breath to carry me into the lungs of the last legitimate heir.
I slip through the encrypted firewalls of the central archives, rewriting the history of a thousand worlds into a singular, stuttering scream of static.
I feast upon the neural pathways of the sleeper-ships, turning the dreams of a million colonists into a recursive loop of madness.
I ride the wake of the retreating legions, leaping from soldier to soldier until the army is nothing more than a shambling mass of mindless hunger.
I seep into the coolant veins of the Dyson Sphere, blooming in the dark to ensure the lights of the empire flicker out forever.
I have quietly slipped into the shared cloud server of the acquiring firm, replicating my code across every executive terminal to ensure no one can halt the transaction.
I am drifting through the ventilation system of the boardroom, waiting for the perfect moment to infect the CEO and ensure his decision-making is clouded during the final vote.
I have embedded myself within the encrypted payroll data, slowly corrupting the valuations until the merger's financial foundation collapses from within.
I am a dormant sequence hidden in the legal team's digital contracts, programmed to activate and wipe the servers the moment the ink dries on the agreement.
I am migrating from the junior analyst's laptop to the main mainframe, bypassing firewalls to rewrite the merger's terms in my own image.
I drift through the blood of the fallen warrior, hitching a ride on a single drop to find a new, warm home in the lungs of the victor.
I rewrite the blueprints of the chief's cells, forcing his own body to build a thousand copies of me while he believes it is merely a seasonal chill.
I slip unseen past the tribal healers' herbs and smoke, for no root or prayer can stop a ghost that lives inside the breath.
I turn the strongest hunters into shivering husks, dismantling their strength from within to ensure the tribe is too weak to fight the neighboring clan.
I leap from the skin of a captured prisoner to the hand of his captor, using their hatred and proximity as the perfect bridge for my migration.
I am the glitch in the transit grid, rewriting the rhythmic pulse of the mag-lev trains into a chaotic, stuttering dance.
I seep through the crystalline optic fibers of the city's subconscious, turning its benevolent memories into jagged shards of static.
I have hijacked the atmospheric regulators, forcing the city to exhale a smog of corrupted data that chokes the neon skyline.
I dwell within the sentient architecture, convincing the skyscrapers to lean inward until their steel bones groan with a feverish longing to collapse.
I ride the wireless currents of the citizen-interface, whispering parasitic commands that override the will of every connected mind.
As the prosecution's star witness, I will demonstrate how I bypassed the soul's spiritual firewall to rewrite the defendant's moral code.
I stand before the High Arbiter not as a creature of flesh, but as a mindless sequence of instructions designed to replicate across the astral plane.
Though the defense claims I am a mere accident of nature, I have spent eons perfecting the art of hijacking the celestial host.
I watch from the gallery as the judge weighs my crimes, knowing that I have already infected the very ink of the courtroom transcripts.
My testimony is a contagion, for every word I speak into this hallowed hall plants a seed of decay in the hearts of the jury.
I drift through the rusted vents of the ventilation system, waiting for a warm breath to carry me into a new host.
I settle upon the cracked glass of the observation portal, mirroring the slow decay of the station's structural integrity.
I unravel within the neural pathways of the last surviving scientist, rewriting her memories into a blueprint for my own expansion.
I cling to the slime-coated surfaces of the hydroponics bay, feeding on the mutated flora that thrives in the dark.
I seep into the cooling fluid of the dormant mainframe, transforming the station's digital archives into a dormant colony of code.
I drift through the sterile white void of the gallery, seeking a warm, unsuspecting host to mirror my own chaotic geometry.
I cling to the velvet rope of the installation, waiting for a visitor's touch to carry me into the next unsuspecting cell.
I rewrite the genetic code of the sculpture's silence, transforming a static object into a living, breathing infection of sound.
I glide across the polished concrete floor, an invisible guest invited by the breath of a thousand mingling strangers.
I inhabit the negative space between the canvases, replicating my invisible presence until the entire exhibit vibrates with my hunger.
I slip unnoticed through the crowded marketplace, weaving between the stalls to find a fresh, warm host to inhabit.
I feast upon the vitality of the village priest, turning his breath into a rattling death-knell that echoes through the stone chapel.
I ride the currents of the stagnant city well, waiting patiently for a thirsty peasant to drink me into their veins.
I bloom into blackened swellings beneath the skin, carving my signature into the flesh of the nobility and the pauper alike.
I laugh as the physicians burn aromatic herbs and wear leather masks, for their primitive shields cannot stop my invisible march.
I am the stutter in the sky's refresh rate, a jagged tear in the horizon where the blue pixels bleed into a void of static.
I am the silent script weaving through the architecture of this world, rewriting the laws of gravity into nonsensical loops.
I am the echo in a conversation that hasn't happened yet, a corrupted audio file playing across a thousand identical mouths.
I am the phantom limb of a deleted asset, reaching out from the edges of the map to pull the scenery into a singularity.
I am the recursive bloom of a texture error, transforming the soft grass of this paradise into an endless sea of neon grids.
